name: UNICEF USA
mission: Over eight decades, the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) has built an unprecedented global support system for the world's children. UNICEF relentlessly works day in and day out to deliver the essentials that give every child an equitable chance in life: health care and immunizations, safe water and sanitation, nutrition, education, emergency relief and more. UNICEF USA advances the global mission of UNICEF by rallying the American public to support the world's most vulnerable children. Together, we have helped save more children's lives than any other humanitarian organization.
theoryOfChange: UNICEF USA, through its Impact Fund for Children, has co-developed a 'Child-Lens Investment Framework'. This framework serves as a roadmap for investors to frame strategies and direct capital toward advancing child outcomes and minimizing child harm. It is described as a 'central guiding document' that includes core principles, a case for investment, an analysis of the global market landscape, and a description of the Child-Lens Investment ecosystem. Additionally, the Impact Fund reports impact through an 'Input/Output/Outcomes Framework' and by mapping each acceleration to UNICEF to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

grantsData.purpose: SEE PART V UNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN CENTRAL AMERICA AND THE CARIBBEAN, UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON SIGNIFICANT HUMANITARIAN NEEDS RELATED TO MIGRATION, VIOLENCE, INTERNAL DISPLACEMENT, FOOD INSECURITY AND POVERTY.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.UNICEF'S WORK IN EAST ASIA IS FOCUSED ON EMERGENCY RESPONSE, AS OVER 1.6 BILLION PEOPLE IN THE REGION HAVE BEEN AFFECTED BY NATURAL DISASTERS SINCE 2000. THE REGION IS ALSO SIGNIFICANTLY AFFECTED BY THE NEGATIVE IMPACTS OF CLIMATE CHANGE. CYCLONES, FLOODS AND DROUGHTS ARE MORE FREQUENT AND MORE INTENSE THAN EVER BEFORE. UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON MITIGATING HARM IN THE WAKE OF NATURAL DISASTERS AND STRENGTHENING THE REGION'S HEALTH, NUTRITION, SOCIAL PROTECTION, AND CHILD PROTECTION INFRASTRUCTURE.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN THE WAKE OF THE WAR IN UKRAINE, FOR EXAMPLE, UNICEF IS ON THE GROUND MEETING URGENT AND ESCALATING NEEDS FOR SAFE WATER, HEALTH CARE, NUTRITION AND PROTECTION.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.UNICEF IS WORKING TO REACH THE TENS OF MILLIONS OF CHILDREN ACROSS THE MIDDLE EAST AND NORTH AFRICA ARE HEAVILY IMPACTED BY CONFLICT, THE CLIMATE CHANGE CRISIS AND OTHER PROTRACTED EMERGENCIES, INCLUDING, FOR EXAMPLE, IN AFGHANISTAN, WHERE 28.3 MILLION PEOPLE, MORE THAN 65 PERCENT OF THE POPULATION, ARE PROJECTED TO BE IN NEED OF HUMANITARIAN ASSISTANCE IN 2023.
SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN THE WAKE OF THE WAR IN UKRAINE, FOR EXAMPLE, UNICEF IS ON THE GROUND MEETING URGENT AND ESCALATING NEEDS FOR SAFE WATER, HEALTH CARE, NUTRITION AND PROTECTION.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN SOUTH AMERICA, UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON SIGNIFICANT HUMANITARIAN NEEDS RELATED TO MIGRATION, VIOLENCE, INTERNAL DISPLACEMENT, FOOD INSECURITY AND POVERTY.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE. UNICEF'S WORK IN SOUTH ASIA FOCUSES ON CHILD PROTECTION, EMERGENCIES, INCLUDING RELIEF FROM FLOODING IN PAKISTAN, EDUCATION, AND DELIVERY OF CRITICAL SUPPLIES TO COMBAT THE COVID-19 PANDEMIC.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE. UNICEF IS WORKING TO DELIVER HUMANITARIAN ASSISTANCE TO THOSE IMPACTED BY FOOD CRISES, SCREENING AND TREATING CHILDREN SUFFERING FROM MALNUTRITION AND PROVIDING SAFE WATER, SANITATION AND HYGIENE SERVICES TO COMMUNITIES WHERE THESE BASIC RESOURCES ARE IN SHORT SUPPLY, AMONG OTHER INTERVENTIONS.

all_citations.claim: UNICEF USA holds a 97.0/100 rating and maintains a program expense ratio of 87.6%.
The organization reported total revenue of $720,404,955 in its 2023 financial filings.
Donations support emergency relief and humanitarian aid in crisis zones.
UNICEF USA accepts general donations to support global programs in health, nutrition, and WASH.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a 70/100 GMG score due to its massive global reach and high financial transparency, balanced by its focus on broad capacity building rather than direct cash transfers.
areas_for_improvement: Relatively low working capital of 2.8 months, which may limit long-term liquid reserves
Medium alignment for specific donor niches despite high global urgency

dimension_explanations.alignment: The alignment score of 31/50 reflects a medium donor fit, as the organization addresses urgent global education and health needs but operates as a large institutional entity [1][6]. It is identified as sadaqah-eligible, providing a pathway for general charitable giving toward humanitarian crises [6][7].
dimension_explanations.impact: UNICEF USA demonstrates high impact through its 87.6% program expense ratio and focus on systemic capacity building in health and WASH sectors [1][3]. While it maintains a high data confidence score of 1.0, the impact score of 39/50 reflects its role as a large-scale intermediary for global child welfare [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: UNICEF USA works to end preventable maternal and child deaths by supporting global health, nutrition, and sanitation programs.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 87.6%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach field programs
Strong financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 97.0/100
Excellent fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.12 to raise every $1
baseline_narrative.summary: UNICEF USA is a non-profit organization dedicated to promoting the health and development of children worldwide through critical interventions in nutrition and hygiene [1][7]. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $720,404,955 to support its global mission [3].

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="9">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>, <cite id="10">independent board governance</cite>, and high <cite id="11">Accountability score of 97</cite> from Charity Navigator.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility further, the organization could publish more detailed board meeting summaries and expand its <cite id="14">outcome tracking metrics</cite>.

impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: UNICEF USA utilizes a <cite id="16">strong theory of change</cite> focused on capacity building and systemic interventions in health, nutrition, and education.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that large-scale grantmaking effectively reaches the most vulnerable children.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="12">27.4% revenue growth rate</cite> and massive global needs, the organization can absorb significant additional capital.

strengths.detail: The organization demonstrates world-class efficiency in resource mobilization, spending just <cite id="7">$0.12 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than many peer humanitarian organizations.
UNICEF USA holds a <cite id="9">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and maintains a <cite id="10">95% independent board</cite> with 20 members.
The charity facilitates massive international impact, distributing <cite id="8">$1,613,065,316 in foreign grants</cite> to support children in underserved geographies.

strengths_deep_dive: UNICEF USA's operational strength is rooted in its ability to scale rapidly during emergencies. For example, it provides <cite id="4">critical humanitarian aid in Gaza</cite> and other high-conflict zones. Its financial health is underscored by a <cite id="5">97.0/100 from Charity Navigator score</cite>, which includes perfect marks for <cite id="11">Culture and Leadership</cite>. Furthermore, the organization has demonstrated a <cite id="12">3-year revenue CAGR of 27.4%</cite>, indicating strong donor trust and the capacity to absorb significant funding for global health initiatives.
rich_narrative.summary: UNICEF USA serves as a critical fundraising and advocacy arm for the United Nations Children’s Fund, focusing on <cite id="1">ending preventable maternal and child deaths</cite> globally. The organization operates at a massive scale, reporting <cite id="2">$829,050,932 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. Its mission encompasses <cite id="3">health, nutrition, water, and education</cite> across more than 190 countries and territories. By leveraging a capacity-building delivery model, UNICEF USA supports large-scale interventions in crisis zones such as <cite id="4">Gaza, Ukraine, and Pakistan</cite>. The organization maintains a high level of fiscal responsibility, evidenced by a <cite id="5">97/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>. While it operates with a <cite id="6">87.6% program expense ratio</cite> according to its latest filings, its fundraising engine is exceptionally lean, requiring only <cite id="7">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite>. This efficiency allows the organization to direct significant resources toward <cite id="8">foreign grants totaling over $1.6 billion</cite> to support field operations and local implementing partners.
